article_title: Rocket Lab Books More Launches in Q1 Than All of 2025, Adds $30M Anduril HASTE Deal, Space Force Interceptor Pick, and Motiv Robotics Buyout
summary: Strong, multi-sourced piece on a busy Q1 day for Rocket Lab. Single recoverable misattribution: the Mynaric acquisition paragraph is attributed to SpaceNews but the term 'Mynaric' does not appear in any of the five cited source snapshots. Filed as a corrections clarification.

findings.details: Article states '[SpaceNews] reports' that Rocket Lab executives described the Mynaric closing as 'progressing well'. The term 'Mynaric' does not appear in any of the five cited snapshots (verified by direct grep on raw HTML). The claim is plausibly true (the Mynaric acquisition is widely reported) but the SpaceNews attribution cannot be verified from the snapshot.

content_preview.title: Rocket Lab Books More Launches in Q1 Than All of 2025, Adds $30M Anduril HASTE Deal, Space Force Interceptor Pick, and Motiv Robotics Buyout
content_preview.summary: Rocket Lab disclosed 31 new launch contracts plus five Neutron flights in the first quarter, then stacked three same-day announcements: an Anduril hypersonics order, a Raytheon-paired Space Force selection, and a Mars-veteran robotics acquisition.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Rocket Lab Corporation's first-quarter 2026 earnings announcement on May 7 was bracketed by a flurry of separate disclosures that, taken together, reframed the company as a defense-and-services contractor as much as a launch provider. Alongside record contract bookings, Rocket Lab disclosed a $30 million HASTE hypersonics contract with Anduril, selection by the U.S. Space Force for a Raytheon-led Space Based Interceptor demonstration, and a deal to acquire Mars-veteran robotics firm...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, well-sourced 791-word News piece pulling together a four-event Rocket Lab news day with clean separation of bookings, hypersonics, missile defense, and robotics acquisition.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 snapshots from disk. SpaceNews (source-0): five-launch confidential customer contract, Neutron+Electron 2026-2029 schedule, $190M/20-launch prior record reference, Motiv Space Systems acquisition, 2026-2029 timing — verified verbatim. Aviation Week #1 (source-1): 31 new launch contracts, Peter Beck attribution, Space Force selection — verified. Aviation Week #2 (source-2): HASTE Launch Complex 2 Virginia, November 2026 first test, Gokul Subramanian attribution — verified. GlobeNewswire HASTE/Anduril (source-3): $30M contract, Mach 5, 100% mission success since 2023, Beck 'speed, affordability, and reliable hypersonic technology testing' verbatim — verified. GlobeNewswire SBI/Raytheon (source-4): Space Force-Raytheon team, Brad Clevenger 'national security priority' attribution — verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All headline and lead claims (31 contracts, $30M Anduril deal, SBI selection, Motiv acquisition) verified verbatim across cited authorities. Single misattribution flagged below.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral; appropriately scoped 'What We Don't Know' on undisclosed deal terms.
concerns: Mynaric acquisition paragraph attributed to SpaceNews ('whose closing Rocket Lab executives described as progressing well [...] [SpaceNews] reports') — but 'Mynaric' is not in the SpaceNews snapshot or any other cited snapshot. The agent's submission report mentions excluding CNBC and Via Satellite for allowlist/bot-block reasons, suggesting Mynaric was picked up from a non-cited source and misattributed.
recommendations: Future submissions: never attribute a claim to a cited source unless the exact claim appears in that source's snapshot. If a fact is real but the canonical source is excluded for allowlist or bot-block reasons, drop the claim or find an allowlisted alternative.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong; the Mynaric misattribution is a single recoverable issue squarely in APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS territory.
